briefdescription.para: Shift the buffer content one line up, exposing a new blank line at the bottom.
The cursor wraps back to the first line of the buffer.
Expand the buffer by one line, keeping the content. Up to maximumSize(), then scroll.
Expand the buffer by one line, keeping the content. Up to maximumSize(), then wrap.
The overflow mode determines what happens when the cursor gets a line-break in the last line.
The overflow mode of the buffer.
The maximum size of the buffer.
The character used for empty cells.
The current position of the cursor.
The current character style.
If auto-wrap is enabled.
If the next character should be wrapped to the next line.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Size startSize, const OverflowMode overflowMode=OverflowMode::Shift, const Size maximumSize=cMaximumSize, const Char fillChar=Char::space())
memberdef.name: CursorBuffer

briefdescription.para: Create a new cursor buffer with the given startSize.

parameterdescription.para: The start size of the buffer.
The overflow mode of the buffer.
The maximum size of the buffer. Only height is used.
The character used to initialize and refill empty cells.
if startSize exceeds the maximum or fillChar is not a single-width character.

briefdescription.para: Create a new cursor buffer with a default size of 80x25 and overflow mode Shift.
Get the maximum size.
Change the maximum size.

detaileddescription.para:
Changing the maximum size will not affect the current content.
maximumSize
The new maximum size.

briefdescription.para: Get the overflow mode for this buffer.
Set the overflow mode for this buffer.
Get the character used for newly exposed or empty cells.
Set the character used for newly exposed or empty cells.

detaileddescription.para:
The character should have a display width of one cell.
fillChar
The new fill character.
std::invalid_argument
if fillChar is not a single-width character.

briefdescription.para: Get the configured size of the buffer.

simplesect.para: The width and height of the buffer.

briefdescription.para: Get the current color.

simplesect.para: The currently tracked terminal color state.

briefdescription.para: Get the current character attributes.

simplesect.para: The currently tracked character attribute state.

briefdescription.para: Set foreground and background color.

simplesect.para: Inherited colors are converted to Default colors.

briefdescription.para: Set all character attributes.

detaileddescription.para:
Unspecified attributes are treated as disabled.
attributes
The new character attributes.

briefdescription.para: Set the foreground color.

simplesect.para: Inherited colors are converted to Default colors.

briefdescription.para: Set the background color.

simplesect.para: Inherited colors are converted to Default colors.

briefdescription.para: Get the character attributes supported by this writer.
Move the cursor absolute or relative.

detaileddescription.para:
If the resulting position is out of bounds, the result is undefined.
posOrDelta
The absolute position or delta for the move.
mode
The move mode, either absolute or relative.

briefdescription.para: Enabled/disable auto-wrap.

detaileddescription.para:
Auto wrap controls if the cursor automatically wraps to the next line when reaching the right margin. This is a feature that can be enabled or disabled. Do not confuse this with line wrapping, which is a different feature.
enabled
Whether to enable or disable auto-wrap.

briefdescription.para: Clears the screen/writing area.
Write a character at the current cursor position.

detaileddescription.para:
Inherited color components resolve against the currently active color. Overwrites the character under the cursor.
character
The character to write.

briefdescription.para: Write a string at the current cursor position.

detaileddescription.para:
Inherited color components in each character resolve against the currently active color. Overwrites the characters under the cursor.
str
The string to write.

briefdescription.para: Write a character that is already fully resolved against the writer state.

detaileddescription.para:
This bypasses any additional inherited-style resolution in implementations that can optimize for it.
character
The already resolved character to write.

briefdescription.para: Write a string whose characters are already fully resolved against the writer state.

detaileddescription.para:
This bypasses any additional inherited-style resolution in implementations that can optimize for it.
str
The already resolved string to write.

briefdescription.para: Write a buffer at the current cursor position.

detaileddescription.para:
This will not perform any additional formatting, clipping, or processing. Each line of the buffer will be written, and a line-break added after each line.
buffer
The buffer to write.

briefdescription.para: Write a line-break.

detaileddescription.para: This will move the cursor to the beginning of the next line.

briefdescription.para: Set foreground and background color.

simplesect.para: Inherited colors are converted to Default colors.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Foreground foregroundColor, const Background backgroundColor) noexcept
memberdef.name: setColor

briefdescription.para: Set foreground and background color.

simplesect.para: Inherited colors are converted to Default colors.

briefdescription.para: Write a character at the current cursor position.

detaileddescription.para:
Inherited color components resolve against the currently active color. Overwrites the character under the cursor.
character
The character to write.

briefdescription.para: Write a string at the current cursor position.

detaileddescription.para:
Inherited color components in each character resolve against the currently active color. Overwrites the characters under the cursor.
str
The string to write.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts. Invalid UTF-8 bytes are replaced with the Unicode replacement character.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
Write a buffer at the current cursor position.

detaileddescription.para:
This will not perform any additional formatting, clipping, or processing. Each line of the buffer will be written, and a line-break added after each line.
buffer
The buffer to write.

briefdescription.para: Write a line-break.

detaileddescription.para: This will move the cursor to the beginning of the next line.

briefdescription.para: Print a paragraph using the cursor-aware buffer output.
A buffer that can be used to write text using a cursor.

detaileddescription.para:
Cursor movement emulates VT100 terminals:
Cursor movements are bound to the buffer (they do not wrap around).
Printing characters do wrap around if wrap mode is enabled (it's enabled by default.)
Printing a character in the last column is special:
A 1-width character is printed in the last column, but the cursor isn't moved yet. A wrapOnNextChar is set instead of the cursor movement.
As soon as the next character is printed, the cursor is moved to the next line first; then the character is printed.
When a 2-width character is printed in the last column, the cursor is moved to the next line first; then the character is printed (not VT100, it didn't have 2-width characters).
If the wrapOnNextChar is set, and the cursor is moved, the flag is cleared.
If the wrapOnNextChar is set, and a line-break is printed, the flag is cleared first before moving the cursor to the next line.
